title: Jesus Is Condemned to Death
med: Consider how Jesus, after having been scourged and crowned with thorns, was unjustly condemned by Pilate to die on the Cross.
stabat_lat:
Stabat Mater dolorósa,
iuxta Crucem lacrimósa,
dum pendébat Fílius.
stabat_eng:
At the Cross her station keeping,
stood the mournful Mother weeping,
close to Jesus to the last.

title: Jesus Carries His Cross
med: Consider how Jesus, bearing His Cross, went forth to the place of sacrifice, like a meek lamb led to the slaughter.
stabat_lat:
Cuius ánimam geméntem,
contristátam et doléntem,
pertransívit gládius.
stabat_eng:
Through her heart, His sorrow sharing,
all His bitter anguish bearing,
now at length the sword had pass'd.

title: Jesus Falls the First Time
med: Consider the first fall of Jesus under the weight of the Cross. The weight of our sins pressed Him to the ground.
stabat_lat:
O quam tristis et afflícta
fuit illa benedícta
Mater Unigéniti!
stabat_eng:
Oh, how sad and sore distress'd
was that Mother highly blest
of the sole-begotten One!

title: Jesus Meets His Blessed Mother
med: Consider the meeting of the Son with His Mother. Their eyes met, and their hearts were pierced with sorrow — hers for His suffering, His for her grief.
stabat_lat:
Quæ mœrébat et dolébat,
pia Mater, dum vidébat
nati pœnas ínclyti.
stabat_eng:
Christ above in torment hangs;
she beneath beholds the pangs
of her dying glorious Son.

title: Simon Helps Carry the Cross
med: Consider how the soldiers, fearing Jesus would die before reaching Calvary, forced Simon of Cyrene to carry the Cross behind Him.
stabat_lat:
Quis est homo qui non fleret,
Matrem Christi si vidéret
in tanto supplício?
stabat_eng:
Is there one who would not weep,
whelm'd in miseries so deep,
Christ's dear Mother to behold?

title: Veronica Wipes the Face of Jesus
med: Consider how the holy woman Veronica, seeing Jesus in such distress, wiped His face with her veil, upon which was left the imprint of His Sacred Face.
stabat_lat:
Quis non posset contristári
Christi Matrem contemplári
doléntem cum Fílio?
stabat_eng:
Can the human heart refrain
from partaking in her pain,
in that Mother's pain untold?

title: Jesus Falls a Second Time
med: Consider the second fall of Jesus beneath the Cross — a fall caused by our repeated sins, our return to evil after repentance.
stabat_lat:
Pro peccátis suæ gentis
vidit Iesum in torméntis,
et flagéllis súbditum.
stabat_eng:
Bruised, derided, curs'd, defiled,
she beheld her tender Child,
all with bloody scourges rent.

title: Jesus Meets the Women of Jerusalem
med: Consider how the pious women wept at the sight of Jesus. He turned to them and said: 'Weep not for Me, but for yourselves and for your children.'
stabat_lat:
Vidit suum dulcem natum
moriéntem, desolátum,
dum emísit spíritum.
stabat_eng:
For the sins of His own nation,
she saw Jesus wracked with torments,
all with scourges rent.

title: Jesus Falls a Third Time
med: Consider the third fall. Jesus, utterly exhausted, falls again beneath the Cross — teaching us never to despair, but to rise again after every fall.
stabat_lat:
Eia, Mater, fons amóris,
me sentíre vim dolóris
fac, ut tecum lúgeam.
stabat_eng:
O thou Mother, fount of love,
touch my spirit from above,
make my heart with thine accord.

title: Jesus Is Stripped of His Garments
med: Consider the violence with which the soldiers stripped Jesus. His inner garments adhered to His torn flesh, renewing every wound.
stabat_lat:
Fac ut árdeat cor meum
in amándo Christum Deum,
ut sibi compláceam.
stabat_eng:
Make me feel as thou hast felt;
make my soul to glow and melt
with the love of Christ my Lord.

title: Jesus Is Nailed to the Cross
med: Consider how Jesus was thrown upon the Cross and nailed through His hands and feet. He offered not a cry of pain, but a prayer for His executioners.
stabat_lat:
Sancta Mater, istud agas,
Crucifíxi fige plagas
cordi meo válide.
stabat_eng:
Holy Mother, pierce me through;
in my heart each wound renew
of my Saviour crucified.

title: Jesus Dies on the Cross
med: Consider how Jesus, after three hours of agony on the Cross, cried out: 'It is finished,' and bowing His head, gave up His spirit.
stabat_lat:
Tui Nati vulneráti,
tam dignáti pro me pati,
pœnas mecum dívide.
stabat_eng:
Let me share with thee His pain,
who for all my sins was slain,
who for me in torments died.

title: Jesus Is Taken Down from the Cross
med: Consider how the sacred body was taken down from the Cross and placed in the arms of His afflicted Mother, who received Him with inexpressible tenderness.
stabat_lat:
Fac me vere tecum flere,
Crucifíxo condolére,
donec ego víxero.
stabat_eng:
Let me mingle tears with thee,
mourning Him who mourn'd for me,
all the days that I may live.

title: Jesus Is Laid in the Tomb
med: Consider how the disciples carried the body of Jesus to the sepulchre, and the stone was rolled before its entrance. There, in silence, we await the Resurrection.
stabat_lat:
Quando corpus moriétur,
fac ut ánimæ donétur
paradísi glória. Amen.
stabat_eng:
While my body here decays,
may my soul Thy goodness praise,
safe in paradise with Thee. Amen.
